message:
fix: avoid DROP TABLE in blocked cache rebuild to prevent page leak (#224)

Replace DROP TABLE + CREATE TABLE in reset_blocked_cache_table with
DELETE FROM when the table already exists. frankensqlite's DROP TABLE
leaves old root pages unreachable, causing integrity_check to report
'Page N: never used' after every import-triggered cache rebuild.

DELETE FROM clears rows while keeping table/index structures intact,
avoiding the page-level operations that leak. The per-entry DELETE+INSERT
pattern in insert_blocked_cache_entries handles any phantom B-tree entries
that fsqlite may retain after bulk DELETE (#215).

Falls back to CREATE TABLE only when the table doesn't exist yet (fresh
DB or recovery scenario). Also exports table_exists as pub(crate) for
use in the rebuild path.

Closes #224.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
